narrative: Favorable US PPI data and Fed Chair Warsh's comments that one-time price changes are not necessarily inflationary pressure the dollar, driving GBP/USD to a session high of 1.3498. The greenback retreats as softer inflation metrics reduce rate hike expectations, while gold lower -0.33%; equities +0.12%; DXY -0.19%; VIX 16.1. The NZD/USD bilateral divergence remains the week's dominant G10 theme, supported by a 1.865% G10 scorecard spread. Watch the Bank of Canada press conference today for CAD direction.
